channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: 45 pro-democracy activists get 4 to 10 years in prison in Hong Kong's biggest national security case
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Forty-five ex-lawmakers and activists were sentenced to four to 10 years in prison Tuesday in Hong Kong’s biggest national security case under a Beijing-imposed law that crushed a once-thriving pro-democracy movement.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG and ZEN SOO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: US envoy arrives in Lebanon to meet with officials about possible cease-fire
item.description: A U.S. envoy has arrived in Beirut to meet with Lebanese officials about a possible cease-fire in the Israel-Hezbollah war.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Concerns grow for imprisoned Nobel Peace Prize laureate Narges Mohammadi's health in Iran
item.description: Imprisoned Nobel Peace Prize laureate Narges Mohammadi has undergone a complex surgery in Iran that saw part of a bone in her right leg removed over cancer fears.
item.dc:creator: Jon Gambrell, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: UK farmers plan to protest at Parliament over a tax hike they say will ruin family farms
item.description: British farmers are descending on Parliament to protest a hike in inheritance tax that they say will deal a “hammer blow” to struggling family farms.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: G20 summit calls for more aid to Gaza and an end to the war in Ukraine
item.description: RIO DE JANEIRO (AP) — Leaders of the world's 20 major economies called for a global pact to combat hunger, more aid for war-torn Gaza and an end to hostilities in the Mideast and Ukraine, issuing a joint declaration Monday that was heavy on generalities but short of details on how to accomplish thos
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE, ELÉONORE HUGHES and DAVID BILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tens of thousands crowd New Zealand's Parliament grounds in support of Māori rights
item.description: As tens of thousands of marchers thronged the streets in New Zealand’s capital, Wellington, the column of people, flags aloft, had the air of a festival or a parade rather than a protest.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Graham-Mclay, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Abuse survivors urge the Vatican to globalize the zero-tolerance policy it approved in the US
item.description: ROME (AP) — Survivors of clergy sexual abuse urged the Vatican on Monday to expand its zero-tolerance policy that it approved for the U.S. Catholic Church in 2002 to the rest of the world, arguing that children everywhere should be protected from predator priests. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli strike kills Hezbollah spokesman in Beirut; at least 30 dead in Gaza airstrikes
item.description: A rare Israeli strike in central Beirut has killed the chief spokesman for the Hezbollah militant group. It was the latest in targeted killings of senior Hezbollah officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Typhoon Man-yi leaves 7 dead in Philippines and worsens crisis from back-to-back storms
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Typhoon Man-yi left at least seven people dead in a landslide, destroyed houses and displaced large numbers of villagers before blowing away from the northern Philippines, worsening the crisis wreaked by multiple back-to-back storms, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US and Philippines sign a pact to secure shared military intelligence and weapons technology
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — The United States and the Philippines signed an agreement on Monday to secure the exchange of highly confidential military intelligence and technology in key weapons the U.S. would provide to Manila.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cruise line offers 4-year cruise to 'skip forward,' in wake of U.S. election results
item.description: One cruise line is offering travelers a customized journey for up to four years, to "skip forward," in the wake of the recent presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 10 newborn babies die in India after fire rips through hospital neonatal unit
item.description: Ten newborn babies were killed in northern India after a fire swept through a neonatal intensive care unit on Friday; 38 newborns were reportedly rescued from the ward.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: EU fines Meta over $840 million for ‘abusive practices’ favoring Facebook Marketplace
item.description: Meta received a fine of over $840 million on Wednesday for breaching the European Union’s antitrust rules as it relates to market domination after three years of legal proceedings.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: US health officials report 1st case of new form of mpox in a traveler
item.description: Health officials said Saturday they have confirmed the first U.S. case of a new form of mpox that was first seen in eastern Congo.
item.dc:creator: Mike Stobbe, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Zelensky says Russia-Ukraine war will end ‘faster’ under Trump
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said the Russia-Ukraine war will end “faster” under President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Man arrested for allegedly trying to smuggle hundreds of tarantulas and centipedes on flight
item.description: A South Korean man was arrested in Peru for allegedly attempting to smuggle hundreds of tarantulas and centipedes on a flight back to Korea.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Lebanon considers cease-fire plan while Israel launches new strikes
item.description: Lebanon is considering a U.S.-backed cease-fire plan while Israel launched new strikes in the area south of Beirut. Senior Lebanon officials are reviewing the proposal and it is based on the U.N. Security Council resolution 1701, The Washington Post reported.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: 2,000-year-old Roman road discovered by archaeologists in London
item.description: An ancient Roman road was recently discovered by archaeologists in London. The 2,000-year-old road was located beneath modern-day Old Kent Road.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump looms large as Biden set to meet China's Xi during Latin America summits
item.description: President Biden is in Latin America for the APEC and G-20 summits, in what is likely his last trip as president. The back-to-back summits come as an impending second Trump presidency is near, which may rattle some world leaders.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel's warfare in Gaza 'consistent with genocide:' UN special committee
item.description: Israel's destruction of vital water, sanitation, and food systems has created a "lethal mix of crises," the report said.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Montana Tucker's Oct. 7 film showcases young victims' strength
item.description: Montana Tucker's film, "The Children of October 7," documents what some of the attacks' youngest victims went through.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Ukrainian troops train for trench warfare near France's WWI battlefields
item.description: Around 2,000 Ukrainian soldiers are training in France's eastern Marne region, where German and French armies battled each other during World War I.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fire kills at least 10 people in Spanish retirement home
item.description: A fire that broke out in a retirement home in the Spanish town of Villafranca de Ebro has left 10 people dead; all of the victims were reportedly elderly people.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran told Biden administration it won't try to assassinate President-elect Trump: report
item.description: Iran reportedly gave the White House its promise that it will not attempt to kill Donald Trump just weeks before the Justice Department charged a murder-for-hire man in a scheme to kill the former president.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump said to lift all military restrictions on Israel on 1st day in office according to reports
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump’s promise could lead to a 60-day cease-fire, allowing Israel to suspend hostilities until military support resumes under the new administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: India's capital introduces stricter anti-pollution measures as toxic smog hides Taj Mahal
item.description: To combat worsening air quality, India's government has banned non-essential construction and encouraged residents to avoid burning coal for heating.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Alex Soros, Jared Kushner reportedly obsessed with Albania
item.description: Alex Soros, whose father is Hungarian, reportedly travels to the capital Tirana so often he has called it “my second home.
item.dc:creator: Paula Froelich, NewsNation

item.title: UN watchdog warns time to 'maneuver' on Iran's nuclear program is shrinking: report
item.description: Rafael Grossi, head of the International Atomic Energy Agency, the U.N. nuclear watchdog, says the window of opportunity to diplomatically engage with Iran is beginning to "shrink.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Driver rams his car into crowd in China, killing 35. Police say he was upset about his divorce
item.description: ZHUHAI, China (AP) — A man who authorities said was upset over his divorce settlement rammed his car into a crowd of people exercising at a sports complex in southern China, killing 35 and severely injuring dozens of others, police said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: NG HAN GUAN and HUIZHONG WU, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US says it will not limit Israel arms transfers after some improvements in flow of aid to Gaza
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration said Tuesday that Israel has made some good but limited progress in increasing the flow of humanitarian aid to Gaza and will not limit arms transfers to Israel as it had threatened to a month ago if the situation had not improved.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, JULIA FRANKEL and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Church of England head Justin Welby resigns over handling of sex abuse scandal
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Archbishop of Canterbury Justin Welby, head of the Church of England and spiritual leader of the global Anglican Communion, resigned Tuesday after an investigation found that he failed to tell police about serial physical and sexual abuse by a volunteer at Christian summer camps as soo
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: President Joe Biden meets with Israel's Isaac Herzog
item.description: Joe Biden met with Israel President Isaac Herzog at the White House on Tuesday, which included a gift from Herzog to Biden.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation
